enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that, whenever necessary, Reserve Bank shall issue directions to
Authorised Persons through A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: Persons through A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Master Direction
issued herewith shall be amended suitably simultaneously.

The Authorised Dealers are required to
comply with the requirement of the tax laws, as applicable.
enriched_requirements.object: requirement of the tax laws, as applicable

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A person resident in India may freely utilise all their eligible assets abroad as
well as income on such assets or sale proceeds thereof received after their
return to India for making any payments or to make any fresh investments
abroad without approval of Reserve Bank, provided the cost of such
investments and/ or any subsequent payments received therefor are met
exclusively out of funds forming part of eligible assets held by them and the
transaction is not in contravention to extant FEMA provisions.
enriched_requirements.object: eligible assets abroad as well as income on such assets or sale proceeds thereof received after their return to India for making any payments or to make any fresh investments abroad without approval of Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such account will be treated as resident bank account for all purposes and all
regulations applicable to a resident bank account shall be applicable.

Cheques, instruments, remittances, cash, card or any other proceeds
belonging to the NRI close relative shall not be eligible for credit to this
account.
enriched_requirements.object: to this account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The NRI close relative shall operate such account only for and on behalf of
the resident for domestic payment and not for creating any beneficial interest
for himself.
enriched_requirements.object: for and on behalf of the resident for domestic payment and not for creating any beneficial interest for himself

conditions:
Where the NRI close relative becomes a joint holder with more than one
resident in such account
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where the NRI close relative becomes a joint holder with more than one
resident in such account, such NRI close relative should be the close relative
of all the resident bank account holders.
enriched_requirements.object: of all the resident bank account holders

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where due to any eventuality, the non-resident account holder becomes the
survivor of such an account, it shall be categorized as Non-Resident Ordinary
Rupee (NRO) account as per the extant regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: Ordinary Rupee (NRO) account as per the extant regulations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 1 FEMA 5/2000-RB dated May 03, 2000 has been replaced with FEMA 5(R)/2016-RB dated April 01, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: replaced with FEMA 5(R)/2016-RB dated April 01, 2016

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Onus will be on the non-resident account holder to keep AD bank informed to
get the account categorized as NRO account and all such regulations as
applicable to NRO account shall be applicable.

The above joint account holder facility may be extended to all types of
resident accounts including savings bank account.
enriched_requirements.object: types of resident accounts including savings bank account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While extending this facility the AD bank should satisfy itself about the actual need
for such a facility and also obtain the following declaration duly signed by the non-
resident account holder:
"I am the joint account holder of SB/FD/RD/Current Account bearing No …….
enriched_requirements.object: actual need for such a facility and also obtain the following declaration duly signed by the non- resident account holder: "I

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
I hereby undertake that I shall not use the proceeds lying in the above
account for any transaction in contravention of the provisions of the Foreign
Exchange Management Act (FEMA) 1999, Rules/Regulations made thereunder and
the related circulars/instructions issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: in the above account for any transaction in contravention of the provisions of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) 1999,

conditions:
if any such transaction is put through the said account in
contravention of the FEMA
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
I
further undertake that if any such transaction is put through the said account in
contravention of the FEMA, 1999 or Rules/Regulations made thereunder, I shall be
held responsible for the same.
enriched_requirements.object: the same

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
I shall intimate my bank in the event of any change in
my Non-resident / Resident status."
4) Meeting of Medical expenses of NRI close relatives by Resident Individuals
Where the medical expenses in respect of NRI close relative (NRI as defined in
regulation 2(vi) of 4 Notification No.
enriched_requirements.object: the event of any change in my Non-resident / Resident status

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: FEMA 16/ 2000- RB dated May 3, 2000, ibid.

(a) Indian companies or their AD Category - I banks are not allowed to issue any
direct or indirect guarantee or create any contingent liability or offer any security in
4 FEMA 5/2000-RB dated May 03, 2000 has been replaced with FEMA 5(R)/2016-RB dated April 01, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: replaced with FEMA 5(R)/2016-RB dated April 01, 2016

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Indian companies or their AD Category - I banks using or establishing structures
which contravene the above shall render themselves liable for penal action as
prescribed under FEMA, 1999.
enriched_requirements.object: penal action as prescribed under FEMA, 1999

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
7) Crystallization of Inoperative Foreign Currency Deposits - Reserve Bank
(Depositor Education and Awareness Fund) Scheme, 2014
With the objective of aligning the instructions in respect of foreign currency accounts
with the Reserve Bank (Depositor Education and Awareness Fund) Scheme, 2014,
Authorised Dealer banks are required to crystallise, that is, convert the credit
balances in any inoperative foreign currency denominated deposit into Indian Rupee,
in the manner indicated below:
enriched_requirements.object: convert the credit balances in any inoperative foreign currency denominated deposit into Indian Rupee, in the manner indicate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) In case a foreign currency denominated deposit with a fixed maturity date
remains inoperative for a period of three years from the date of maturity of the
deposit, at the end of the third year, the authorised bank shall convert the
balances lying in the foreign currency denominated deposit into Indian Rupee
at the exchange rate prevailing as on that date.
enriched_requirements.object: in the foreign currency denominated deposit into Indian Rupee at the exchange rate prevailing as on that date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Thereafter, the depositor shall
be entitled to claim either the said Indian Rupee proceeds and interest
thereon, if any, or the foreign currency equivalent (calculated at the rate
prevalent as on the date of payment) of the Indian Rupee proceeds of the
original deposit and interest, if any, on such Indian Rupee proceeds.
enriched_requirements.object: either the said Indian Rupee proceeds and interest thereon

conditions:
In case of foreign currency denominated deposit with no fixed maturity
period

if the deposit remains inoperative for a period of three years (debit of
bank charges not to be reckoned as operation)
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) In case of foreign currency denominated deposit with no fixed maturity
period, if the deposit remains inoperative for a period of three years (debit of
bank charges not to be reckoned as operation), the authorised bank shall,
after giving a three month notice to the depositor at his last known address as
available with it, convert the deposit from the foreign currency in which it is
denominated to Indian Rupee at the end of the notice period at the prevailing
exchange rate.
enriched_requirements.object: three month notice to the depositor at his last known address as available with it, convert the deposit from the foreign currency in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Thereafter, the depositor shall be entitled to claim either the
said Indian Rupee proceeds and interest thereon, if any, or the foreign
currency equivalent (calculated at the rate prevalent as on the date of
payment) of the Indian Rupee proceeds of the original deposit and interest, if
any, on such Indian Rupee proceeds.
enriched_requirements.object: either the said Indian Rupee proceeds and interest thereon

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, their transaction
with a person resident in India will be treated as a transaction between a resident
and non- resident and shall be subject to the provisions of Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999 and the Rules/ Regulations/ Directions issued thereunder.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 and the Rules/ Regulations/ Directions issued thereunder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The financial transaction in this context shall mean making or receiving payment,
drawing, issuing or negotiating any bills of exchange or promissory note, transferring
any security or acknowledging any debt.
enriched_requirements.object: payment, drawing, issuing or negotiating any bills of exchange or promissory note, transferring any security or acknowledging

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, financial service shall mean any
activity which a financial institution is permitted to carry on by the respective Act of
the Parliament or Government of India or any Regulatory Authority empowered to
regulate the concerned financial institution.
enriched_requirements.object: a financial institution is permitted to carry on by the respective Act of the Parliament or Government of India or any Regulat

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
9) Regularisation of assets held abroad by a person resident in India under
Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999
To effectively deal with assets held abroad by persons resident in India in violation of
the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (FEMA) for which declarations have
been made and taxes and penalties have been paid under the provisions of the
Black Money (Undisclosed Foreign Income and Assets) and Imposition of Tax Act,
2015, it is clarified that:
a) No proceedings shall lie under the Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999 (FEMA) against the declarant with respect to an asset held abroad for
which taxes and penalties under the provisions of Black Money Act have been
paid.
enriched_requirements.object: Exchange Management Act, 1999 (FEMA) against the declarant with respect to an asset held abroad for which taxes and penalties un

conditions: In case the declarant wishes to hold the asset so declared
enriched_requirements.timeline:
within 180 days from the date of
declaration if such permission is necessary as on date of application
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
c) In case the declarant wishes to hold the asset so declared, she/ he may
apply to the Reserve Bank of India within 180 days from the date of
declaration if such permission is necessary as on date of application.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India within 180 days from the date of declaration if such permission is necessary as on date of application

conditions: In case such permission is not granted
enriched_requirements.timeline:
within 180 days from the date of receipt of the communication
from the Reserve Bank conveying refusal of permission or within such
extended period as may be permitted by the Reserve Bank and proceeds
brought back to India immediately through the banking channel
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case such permission is not granted, the asset will have to be
disposed of within 180 days from the date of receipt of the communication
from the Reserve Bank conveying refusal of permission or within such
extended period as may be permitted by the Reserve Bank and proceeds
brought back to India immediately through the banking channel.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank and proceeds brought back to India immediately through the banking channel

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Prior to omission, it read as follows (the words in italics were inserted on July 28, 2017, and words underlined were inserted with effect from September 07, 2018):
Operating framework for facilitating Outward Remittance services by non-bank entities through Authorized Dealer
(Category I) banks in India
The non-bank entities may obtain specific approval for each tie-up arrangement from the Reserve Bank for facilitating outward
remittance services through Authorized Dealer (Category I) banks in India to effect outward remittances.
enriched_requirements.object: each tie-up arrangement from the Reserve Bank for facilitating outward remittance services through Authorized Dealer (Cat

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Authorized Dealer (Category I) bank through which the service is being offered shall be responsible for ensuring
that each outward remittance transaction is in compliance with the provisions of governing regulations in India.
enriched_requirements.object: that each outward remittance transaction is in compliance with the provisions of governing regulations in India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The said Authorized Dealer (Category I) bank shall be responsible for ensuring compliance to KYC/ AML standards/
CFT issued by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: compliance to KYC/ AML standards/ CFT issued by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The remittances facilitated under this model shall comprise small value transactions, not exceeding USD 5000 per
transaction, except for overseas education where the limit shall be USD 10000 per transaction.
enriched_requirements.object: not exceeding USD 5000 per transaction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Only current account transactions, in the nature of personal remittances, shall be permitted under this model.
enriched_requirements.object: model

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The remittances shall be permitted only for fund transfers from one bank account to another bank account.
enriched_requirements.object: fund transfers from one bank account to another bank account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Remittances shall be only made to beneficiaries in jurisdictions which are FATF compliant.
enriched_requirements.object: beneficiaries in jurisdictions which are FATF compliant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The remitting service provider shall be a duly licensed entity by regulator of destination jurisdictions to facilitate
remittances to beneficiaries in such jurisdictions.
enriched_requirements.object: entity by regulator of destination jurisdictions to facilitate remittances to beneficiaries in such jurisdictions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The remitter's moneys should be kept distinct from service provider's operating account and such moneys should be
duly protected from insolvency risks of the facilitating service provider.
enriched_requirements.object: service provider's operating account and such moneys should be duly protected from insolvency risks of the facilitating service

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The onus of ensuring the security of the
remitters funds shall be on the Authorized Dealer (Category I) bank.
enriched_requirements.object: Dealer (Category I) bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The Authorized Dealer (Category I) bank may submit to the Reserve Bank every year a certificate stating that the conditions prescribed in the approval are adhered to.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank every year a certificate stating that the conditions prescribed in the approval are adhered to
